title: Root Package Footprint Review
goal: Implement #487 by making the root package dependency-footprint decision explicit and discoverable without changing packaging behavior.
non_goals: Do not split packages or make optional extras in this slice.
Do not break uvx/pipx root lifecycle ergonomics.
Do not implement third-party module loading; #490/#510 own that direction.

intent.outcome: Routing-only/minimal claims are accurate at both repo-state and Python-package levels, and the current first-party module dependency tradeoff is recorded.
intent.constraints: Stay on current branch; preserve root command behavior and packaging dependencies.
intent.latitude: Add decision metadata to existing module registry/default docs and tests.
intent.escalation: Stop before changing pyproject dependencies or lifecycle invocation behavior.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, commit, close #487, refresh external evidence, then continue #485 with the next routed child.
execution.proof: Run contract tooling, focused workspace CLI tests, ruff/docs-adjacent checks, proof selector, and planning closeout.

invariants: Root lifecycle keeps a simple installed path to planning and memory modules.
Routing-only is described as smallest checked-in repo footprint, not smallest Python dependency footprint.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Keep the root orchestrator thin, boring, generic, and strictly bounded.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #487 closes, reingest and promote the next module-boundary-compression child.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Later optional-module or generic-manifest work can start from an explicit package-footprint decision.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Changing pyproject dependencies, optional extras, lazy imports, and third-party module discovery.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: schema-backed package-footprint decision metadata is emitted by the modules command and asserted by tests.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Run proof selector and closeout checks after archive.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #488 or #490 depending on refreshed issue priority.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Review root package dependency footprint and first-party module coupling.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make the current coupling honest and bounded so minimal-footprint claims do not overstate what routing-only means.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add schema-backed package-footprint decision metadata and README/test coverage.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Review whether the decision should remain temporary until #490 or be treated as intentional.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Module registry contract/schema, modules command rendering, README, focused tests, planning surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: contract tooling, focused tests, full workspace CLI tests if cli.py changes, ruff, proof selector, planning checks.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before changing dependencies or module invocation.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: pyproject dependency changes, generated packages, module lifecycle internals, third-party discovery implementation.

stop_conditions.stop when: Package-footprint decision is explicit and tests assert routing-only package-vs-repo footprint language.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The review shows dependencies must change now rather than later.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The work becomes optional extras, lazy loading, or module-manifest redesign.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Contract parity or modules command output drifts unexpectedly.

context_budget.live working set: pyproject.toml, README.md, module_registry.json/schema, modules command tests, issue #487.
context_budget.recoverable later: Full package split and generic module manifest design.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Record any required dependency change as follow-up issue if discovered.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Not needed unless compact output points to Memory.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Decision/metadata slice only; do not change dependencies.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Dependency edits, import failures, or broad module loader changes.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make the root package dependency footprint decision explicit for #487.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Preserve current packaging and root lifecycle ergonomics.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact metadata shape and README wording.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A packaging behavior change appears necessary.

active_milestone.scope: Record dependency-footprint decision metadata and tests.
active_milestone.ready: #487 is the selected next child for #485.

immediate_next_action: Patch package-footprint metadata, README wording, and focused tests.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py
uv run ruff check src tests
completion_criteria: Dependency-footprint and module-coupling tradeoff is explicitly decided.
If unconditional dependencies remain, the reason is recorded and bounded.
Routing-only/minimal install claims are accurate at repo and Python package levels.
The chosen path does not block #490 generic module declarations.

execution_run.what happened: Added schema-backed package-footprint metadata to the module registry, exposed it through the modules command, clarified README footprint wording, and asserted the contract in CLI tests.
execution_run.scope touched: README, module registry contract/schema, modules command output, focused tests, and planning closeout.
execution_run.validations run: contract tooling, focused modules command test, full workspace CLI tests, ruff over src/tests, planning surface check.
execution_run.result for continuation: #487 is satisfied; #488, #490, and #510-#514 remain routed under #485.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan, commit, close #487, refresh external evidence, then continue the module-boundary-compression lane.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no dependency, packaging, or module-loader behavior changed.
finished_run_review.proof status: passed local validation; proof selector and closeout checks remain before commit.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; routing-only and root package footprint are now explicitly separated.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used package summary/check/proof workflow and kept repo invocation through uv.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; the decision is marked intentional-temporary and bounded by follow-up issues.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: archive this slice but keep #485 open through routed children.

proof_report.validation proof: Local checks pass for contract tooling, focused modules command output, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, and planning surfaces.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: modules payload now includes package_footprint, tests assert decision/bounds/wording, and README clarifies checked-in repo footprint versus Python package dependencies.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The root package dependency-footprint decision is explicit, discoverable through `agentic-workspace modules --format json`, schema validated, and documented in README.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Root orchestrator should be thin and honest about footprint while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Record the decision before changing package topology.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this reduce ambiguity for later #490 module-manifest work?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane module-boundary-compression activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Package-footprint decision captured as machine-readable module-registry metadata and exposed in command output.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and full CLI tests plus lint/planning/contract checks pass.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Archived execplan plus schema-backed contract metadata.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Durable product decision lives in module_registry.json and README; no Memory update needed.
execution_summary.resume from: Reingest open issues and choose the next #485 child after closing #487.

discard.summary: Command-by-command execution detail stays in the archived proof record.

memory.summary: Durable product decision lives in module_registry.json and README; no Memory update needed.

docs.summary: README clarifies routing-only as checked-in repo footprint, not Python dependency footprint.
